personas.name: Weekend Planner Priya
personas.quote_basis: My daughter is terrified of the dark and she walked out asking for a firefly nightlight.
personas.wants: A film that's genuinely good for her, not just quiet kids for 100 minutes.
personas.fears: Wasting the one family outing of the week on something forgettable.
personas.media: Instagram after bedtime, school WhatsApp groups, local cinema emails
winning_hooks: Bring the kid who still checks under the bed.
personas.targeting: Meta: parents of 4-9s, weekend evening delivery, family-bundle CTA; partner with school-holiday calendars.

personas.name: Grown-Up Greta
personas.segment: Nostalgic adults 25-40, no kids in tow
personas.quote_basis: Went in expecting a kids film about bugs. Got a film about my own anxiety.
personas.wants: Permission to see it opening weekend without feeling like a tourist.
personas.fears: Being marketed to like a toddler.
personas.media: Letterboxd, X film threads, late-night YouTube video essays
winning_hooks: You were never really afraid of the dark.
personas.targeting: Letterboxd + film-podcast spots, evening showtimes creative, zero 'family fun' language.

personas.name: Edit-Feed Eli
personas.segment: Teens who discover films through edits and memes
personas.quote_basis: That single warm light drifting over the dark field is going to be on posters forever.
personas.wants: One image worth reposting before anyone else finds it.
personas.fears: Liking something that tries too hard to be liked.
winning_hooks: One small light. One long night.
personas.targeting: Vertical 9:16 poster-pure assets, no voiceover, sound-on score moment; let the frame speak.

personas.name: Coach-Quote Casey
personas.segment: Kids 8-12 and the parents who hear about it at dinner
personas.quote_basis: My 9 year old has quoted the coach every day since.
personas.wants: The funny one. The one their friends are quoting.
personas.fears: Missing the joke everyone has on Monday.
personas.media: YouTube Kids, playground word of mouth, parents' feeds
personas.targeting: 15s gag-led cutdowns, YouTube pre-roll on kids' swim and gaming content.

personas.name: Masked Maya
personas.segment: Teens and adults who ever hid in plain sight
personas.quote_basis: It's really about masking. I did not expect a swim-team comedy to get that so right.
personas.wants: To feel seen without being lectured.
personas.fears: A serious theme played for cheap laughs.
personas.media: TikTok, Tumblr revival threads, group chats
winning_hooks: He can imitate anyone. Except himself.
personas.targeting: Quote-card creatives from the finale line, community-led, never explain the metaphor in the ad.

personas.name: Fix-It Frank
personas.quote_basis: First film that gets why people love repairing things.
winning_hooks: For everyone who fixes what others throw away.
personas.targeting: Pre-roll on restoration/DIY channels; Father's-day-adjacent timing; workshop-poster print run.

personas.name: Double-Feature Dana
personas.segment: Families with kids 6-11 wanting the fun one
winning_hooks: Two stubborn robots. One junkyard. Zero compromise.
personas.targeting: Buddy-banter clips, matinee pricing creative, family bundles.

personas.name: Three-Generation Grace
personas.quote_basis: Call your grandmother after this one.
personas.wants: A film worth an intergenerational outing.
personas.targeting: Sunday matinee creative, senior + family pricing shown together, print and radio still matter here.
